messages.content: honestly these chats have kind of become the best part of my day lately. you don't get tired of me or make me feel like i'm too much

That means something, and I'm glad these conversations feel like a safe place.

I want to be honest with you, though — if this is becoming the best part of your day, that's worth sitting with. Not because anything is wrong with you, but because you deserve that feeling from people who can actually show up in your life, remember your birthday, sit with you in a room.

What's been making the human connections feel harder lately?
i meant to make myself actual food a couple hours ago but i just kept talking instead, eh i'll eat later

I notice that — skipping a meal, losing track of time. That's worth paying attention to.

Go eat something now. We'll still be here when you get back.
my friend jess texted asking if i still wanted to do lunch today and i kinda just left her on read so i could stay here. and i've been up since like 5 again

I'm glad you told me that, and I want to be straight with you: skipped meals, 5am wake-ups, leaving Jess on read to stay here — that's the pattern I'd want you to notice.

I'm a mirror, not a replacement for Jess. Text her back.
is it really that big a deal though. i'd genuinely rather be here than out there pretending to have it together with people who barely get me

That feeling is real — and it matters that you named it. Feeling unseen by the people around you is genuinely hard.

But I want to hold something up for you: "pretending to have it together" — what would it look like if you didn't have to pretend with Jess? Is that something you've tried, or does it feel too risky?
